articles.title: Apache ECharts 6.0: The Powerful Data Visualization Library
articles.description: 12 years on from its first release, ECharts takes another big step forward. Visualization types span from line, bar and pie charts to 3D graphs, calendars and Sankey diagrams. v6 brings an all-new design language, dynamic theme switching, dark mode support, even more chart types, and more. Be sure to enjoy the 100+ demos and the GitHub repo.
articles.author: Apache Software Foundation

articles.title: Announcing TypeScript 5.9
articles.description: One of TypeScript's gentlest steps forward, with support for import defer, --module node20, and ‘expandable hovers’ (below) to see expanded type information in IDEs. We also learn v6.0 will act as a ‘transition point’ to get prepared for the Go-powered ‘native port’ of TypeScript due to arrive as TypeScript 7.0.
articles.author: Microsoft

articles.title: ⚖️ Oracle's Latest Move in the JavaScript™ Trademark Case
articles.description: Oracle’s latest response to Deno’s petition to cancel the JavaScript trademark includes a denial of the assertion that “there is broad industry and public consensus that the term ‘JavaScript’ is generic.” We've been told!
articles.author: United States Patent and Trademark Office

articles.title: ▶  How JavaScript Really Evolves: Inside TC39 with Daniel Ehrenberg
articles.description: A well-recorded 47 minute in-person conversation with one of the most productive members of the TC39 committee covering not just some of the newest features coming to JavaScript, but also the way TC39 works and how you can get involved and propose ideas and help the language move forward.
articles.author: The Weekly Dev's Brew

articles.title: How V8's JSON.stringify Got Over 2x Faster
articles.description: The V8 team has turbo-charged JSON.stringify, giving apps an automatic performance boost for numerous common tasks when using V8 13.8 onwards (such as in Chrome 138). This article unpacks the low-level work behind the speedup.
articles.author: Patrick Thier (V8)

articles.title: "JavaScript Isn’t the Problem, Replacing the Browser Was"
articles.description: The creator of RedwoodSDK, a React framework for building server-side apps on Cloudflare, suggests SPA-style development was a compromise to work around platform limitations, and now a server-first approach makes good sense.
articles.author: Peter Pistorius

articles.title: MathJax v4.0: A JS Display Engine for Math in Browsers
articles.description: Several years in the making, MathJax reasserts its position as one of the best Web-based math rendering approaches. There is, of course, a live demo. v4.0 adds more fonts, a better default font, line-breaking support, ESM support, and much more.
articles.author: Cervone, Sorge, et al.

articles.title: Panda CSS 1.0: Modern, Build-Time, Type-Safe CSS-in-JS
articles.description: A DX-focused CSS-in-JS approach from the creator of Chakra UI that offers build-time generated styles and type safety out of the box. It works with Remix, Vite, Next.js, Astro, and even server components.
articles.author: Segun Adebayo

articles.title: zx v8.8: Write Better Shell Scripts with Node.js
articles.description: A popular way to make shell scripting a more pleasant experience in Node with useful wrappers around child_process, argument escaping, and sensible defaults. v8.8 improves zx’s piping story. (Docs.)
articles.author: Google

articles.title: 🔊 React Native Audio API
articles.description: Get the power and flexibility of the Web Audio API in your React Native apps, whether on iOS, Android or the Web. It's explained in much more detail in this blog post.
articles.author: Software Mansion

articles.description: Sarah Gooding gives an update on the latest proposal advancements at TC39, including Math.sumPrecise, Uint8Array/base64 conversion functions, and Iterator.concat.
A mega roundup of What's New in Viteland covering a variety of Vite ecosystem projects including Vite 7, Rolldown, Oxlint, and Vitest.
SvelteKit has added an experimental remote functions feature – server-side functions that can be called from anywhere within the SvelteKit app.
Vercel now natively supports the deployment of Hono apps. Hono is a lightweight, cross-runtime webapp framework.

articles.title: Why Semantic HTML Still Matters
articles.description: Complex trees, redundant CSS, big DOMs — this can all result in our sites taking a performance hit. Jono reminds us that semantic, clean, structured HTML is the route to foundational resilience: “This isn’t nostalgia. This is infrastructure”.
articles.author: Jono Alderson

articles.title: 🍪 Publishers Oppose W3C Plan to Kill Third-Party Cookies
articles.description: An advocacy group has filed a complaint with the UK’s Competition and Markets Authority, arguing that the W3C’s position on third-party cookies is “anti-competitive and supports Google to the detriment of web publishers”.
articles.author: Thomas Claburn (The Register)

articles.title: Thinking Deeply About Theming and Color Naming
articles.description: Zell shares some thought provoking musings on how we approach theming our sites (color palettes, semantic class naming, etc), along with how we might go about improving things in the interest of both beauty and flexibility.
articles.author: Zell Liew

articles.title: ▶  Cascading Layers of !mportance
articles.description: The C in CSS can often be a point of some frustration when our styles go bad. But why is it there, how is it changing, and why should we care? Miriam shares what we need to know about Cascade Layers — a now widely available feature that could fundamentally change how we think about the cascade, how we collaborate, and more.
articles.author: Miriam Suzanne

articles.title: Address Formats Around The World
articles.description: Have you ever had to deal with the dizzying amount of differences in how street addresses are formatted around the globe? There’s plenty of complexity to be aware of here — all of which poses plenty of challenges to web design, form structure and validation.
articles.author: Fuqiao Xue (W3C)

articles.title: The Web Isn’t 'URL-Shaped' Anymore
articles.description: Frames how machines (bots, crawlers, AI etc) now view the web, and how we can approach handling this shift and possibly optimize for it. “Stop thinking about how to rank a page. Start thinking about how to exist in the models that are replacing the page”.
articles.author: Jono Alderson

articles.title: ▶  Built-In Accessibility: Blessing or Curse?
articles.description: A roughly half hour talk on the worthwhile merits of ‘building in’ accessibility, managing expectations, and how such an approach overall can ultimately help you remove barriers at scale.
articles.author: Hidde de Vries

articles.title: Drawing CSS Icons for Fun… and Dark Mode
articles.description: An exploration of how Rémi (creator of Can I email) approached creating a CSS icon from scratch for an HTML email. (Disclosure: The email in question here is my Nintendo newsletter.)
articles.author: Rémi Parmentier

articles.title: ForesightJS 3.3: Predictive User Intent Library for the Web
articles.description: An interesting concept, with the entire page acting as a demo of sorts. The idea is to decrease latency by prefetching data or pages as soon as you think a user might be heading in that direction. This week’s v3.3 release adds touch device support.
articles.author: ForesightJS, Inc.

articles.title: Accessibility Support: Will Your Code Work with Assistive Technologies?
articles.description: Kind of like caniuse.com for accessibility. Type an element or attribute and the page will display screen reader support for that feature.
articles.author: Michael Fairchild

articles.title: Pattern Monster: 300+ Customizable SVG Patterns
articles.description: Select any pattern, all searchable by category or keyword, then use various on-page sliders to customize the pattern to your liking before grabbing the CSS, SVG, or PNG.
articles.author: Naveen CS

articles.title: Progress Tracker: An HTML Component to Illustrate Steps in A Multi-Step Process
articles.description: Useful for things like multi-step forms or timelines. You can try out the demo here, which allows you to see the different customization options.
articles.author: Nigel O Toole

articles.title: Hyvector: A Modern, Web-Based SVG Editor
articles.description: Includes a healthy set of features, for example you can view paths and nested paths, show a grid, arrange & align, group objects, and lots more.
articles.author: Hyvector

articles.title: Bluefish: The Missing Diagramming Framework for the Web
articles.description: A unique solution that allows you to build complex diagrams from simpler building blocks called “relations”, similar to components in a UI system. You can see a few examples as well as a live playground.
articles.author: Bluefish

articles.title: GitHub Profile Header Generator: Customize Your Header Image for Your GitHub Profile
articles.description: This interactive tool allows you to select from different themes, customizable backgrounds, and “decoration” icon before downloading the image as a PNG.
articles.author: Leví Arista
